title: Falls Prevention for Skilled Nursing Facilities

seo.title: Falls Prevention for Skilled Nursing | Able Care
seo.description: Standardized falls screening for skilled nursing facilities. Protect Five-Star ratings, defend against CMS audits, and build the case load — all in under five minutes per patient.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Therapist conducting a bedside Able Assess screening with an SNF patient
blocks.headline: Standardized falls screening for skilled nursing.
blocks.subtitle: Protect Five-Star ratings. Defend against CMS audits. Build the case load. One standardized screen across every site and every patient — usable by any member of staff.

blocks.heading: What Able Assess Offers Skilled Nursing

metrics.name: Standardization & quality
metrics.whatItMeasures: One standard assessment for every patient, across every staff member and every site. Defensible documentation that falls risk was considered and addressed — critical if the SNF is audited.

metrics.name: Build the case load
metrics.whatItMeasures: Inside a CCRC, screen non-SNF residents to identify candidates for Part B PT. In a standalone SNF, screen patients without a full evaluation — opening the door to more patients on service.

metrics.name: Five-Star and VBP protection
metrics.whatItMeasures: Falls outcomes flow into the Care Compare quality measures that drive your Star Ratings and CMS reimbursement. Objective screening protects both.

blocks.heading: How it works in skilled nursing

steps.title: Capture
steps.description: PT, nursing or other staff run a five-minute screen at admission. The protocol satisfies the Medicare requirement for objective testing at intake.

steps.title: Score
steps.description: Grip strength, sit-to-stand, gait speed and timed up-and-go are scored against normative data instantly. Grip strength can be captured even when the patient can't complete CST, TUG or 4MGS.

steps.title: Act
steps.description: The risk report informs the plan of care, evidences improvement throughout the stay and supports defensible documentation for SNF PPS Section GG reporting.

steps.title: Track
steps.description: Re-screen at discharge. Objective evidence of improvement feeds Care Compare quality measures, Star Ratings and the conversations with referral sources.

blocks.heading: Four metrics, one picture of function

metrics.name: Grip strength
metrics.whatItMeasures: Full body strength proxy. Can be captured even when a patient can't complete CST, TUG or 4MGS — every patient gets a score.

metrics.name: Sit-to-stand
metrics.whatItMeasures: Lower-limb power and balance. A key predictor of discharge readiness and safe transfer ability.

metrics.name: Gait speed
metrics.whatItMeasures: The 'sixth vital sign.' Linked to rehospitalization risk, length of stay and VBP outcomes.

metrics.name: Timed Up and Go
metrics.whatItMeasures: Falls risk in under a minute. Validated in skilled nursing and rehabilitation settings.

blocks.heading: The numbers that matter to skilled nursing

stats.label: Reduction in falls with objective screening
Clinical certifications required to run it

testimonials.quote: We caught decline six weeks earlier than we would have with our old process.
The objective data gave us something defensible to show surveyors. That alone was worth the investment.

blocks.heading: Common questions from skilled nursing operators

faqs.question: How does Able Assess help protect Five-Star ratings?
faqs.answer: Falls outcomes feed the Care Compare quality measures that directly impact Five-Star ratings — and new CMS regulation has increased penalties for falls. Objective screening identifies at-risk residents earlier, enabling timely intervention before falls occur and protecting your quality scores.

faqs.question: Can non-clinical staff run the screening?
faqs.answer: Yes. Able Assess can be used by any staff — clinical training isn't required. The workflow guides them through every step.

faqs.question: How does it support a CMS audit?
faqs.answer: Able Assess creates a defensible paper trail showing that falls risk was considered and addressed for every patient — critical if the SNF is audited.

faqs.question: How does it work inside a CCRC?
faqs.answer: Use Able Assess to screen non-SNF residents (IL/AL) and identify those who could benefit from Part B PT — getting more patients onto the case load.

faqs.question: What about patients who cannot stand or walk on admission?
faqs.answer: Grip strength can be assessed seated or supine. Every patient gets a score, even when CST, TUG or 4MGS aren't possible at intake.

faqs.question: What does it cost?
faqs.answer: Unlimited tests, unlimited users — no per-patient or per-test charge. Volume discounts available for larger or multi-site customers. Book a demo to discuss your scenario.

blocks.heading: See Able Assess in your skilled nursing workflow.
blocks.bodyText: Book a walkthrough with a clinician who has deployed it in SNF settings.
